title: REvil Lab (CyberDefenders)

headings.text: Analysis of File Creation (Sysmon Event Code 11)
Analysis of Process Creation (Sysmon Event Code 1)
Analysis of Network Connections (Sysmon Event Code 3)
Analysis of Registry Modifications (Sysmon Event Code 13)
Analysis of Process Termination (Sysmon Event Code 5)

overview: Hello these are my notes taken while solving the lab, not a polished writeup. I was practicing for the ECIR exam at the time and plan to revisit the lab to add screenshots and turn this into a full write-up in the future.

study.overview: Hello these are my notes taken while solving the lab, not a polished writeup. I was practicing for the ECIR exam at the time and plan to revisit the lab to add screenshots and turn this into a full write-up in the future.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: Analysis of File Creation (Sysmon Event Code 11)
Analysis of Process Creation (Sysmon Event Code 1)
Analysis of Network Connections (Sysmon Event Code 3)
Analysis of Registry Modifications (Sysmon Event Code 13)

workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
